questions.question: What is a mineral?
questions.correct_answer: A naturally occurring homogeneous substance with a definable internal structure
questions.explanation: Minerals are basic components of rocks and are found in the earth’s crust.

questions.question: What is an ore?
questions.correct_answer: A mineral that contains enough metal to make extraction economically possible
questions.explanation: Ores are mined because they contain valuable minerals in sufficient concentration.

questions.question: What are ferrous minerals?
questions.explanation: Ferrous minerals such as iron ore and manganese are important for steel production.

questions.question: What are non-ferrous minerals?
questions.correct_answer: Minerals that do not contain iron
questions.explanation: Examples include copper, bauxite, lead, zinc and gold.

questions.question: Which mineral is the basic raw material for the cement industry?
questions.explanation: Limestone is widely used in cement manufacturing and in smelting iron ore.

questions.question: Which mineral is widely used in electrical and electronic industries due to its insulating properties?
questions.explanation: Mica has excellent insulating properties and resistance to high voltage.

questions.question: Which fossil fuel is the most abundantly available energy resource in India?
questions.explanation: Coal is widely used for power generation and industrial energy.

questions.question: What are conventional sources of energy?
questions.correct_answer: Traditional energy sources such as coal, petroleum, natural gas, firewood and electricity
questions.explanation: These sources have been used for a long time to generate energy.

questions.question: What are non-conventional sources of energy?
questions.correct_answer: Renewable energy sources such as solar, wind, tidal, geothermal and biogas
questions.explanation: They are environmentally friendly and help reduce dependence on fossil fuels.

questions.question: Why is energy conservation important?
questions.correct_answer: Because many energy resources are limited and non-renewable
questions.explanation: Efficient use of energy ensures availability for future generations.

notes.title: Minerals
notes.content: Minerals are naturally occurring substances found in rocks and are essential for industrial and economic development.

notes.title: Ferrous Minerals
notes.content: Ferrous minerals contain iron and are essential for metallurgical industries and steel production.

notes.title: Non-Ferrous Minerals
notes.content: Non-ferrous minerals such as copper and bauxite are important for electrical and engineering industries.

notes.title: Conventional Energy Sources
notes.content: Coal, petroleum, and natural gas are major conventional sources used for power generation and industries.

notes.title: Renewable Energy
notes.content: Renewable energy sources like solar and wind are sustainable alternatives to fossil fuels.

facts.fact: Iron ore is the backbone of industrial development.
India’s major petroleum production areas include Mumbai High, Gujarat and Assam.
Coal is formed from plant material compressed over millions of years.
Solar energy is abundant in India due to its tropical location.
